page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong CPU performance. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both rank in the top 25 percent, with scores of 2849.98 and 67555.75 respectively. Passmark CPU physics testing also shows strong performance at 7022.49. Memory bandwidth tests fall within the average range, yielding 141.36 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 71.93 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations and Redis SET performance are average, with Redis handling 42.05 million operations per second.

The server utilizes shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated resources under sustained peak loads. However, the high memory density of 192.0 GB paired with a 1200 GB NVMe SSD offers a resource-dense configuration for memory-heavy workloads. This makes the instance suitable for large-scale database hosting, memory-intensive caching layers, data processing pipelines, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It features a high-density memory configuration of 192.0 GB RAM (8.0 GB per core) and includes a bundled 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, with Redis handling 42.05 million operations per second. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained peak workloads, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage make this server well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong CPU performance for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: An AMD EPYC cloud server featuring high memory density and bundled NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
